text.title: Conflict Resolution Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Message reads 'I feel attacked, ignored, manipulated.' Reader hears accusation, not feeling.
antipattern.root-cause: Author used 'I feel' to camouflage accusation.
antipattern.fix: Replace with real feelings: scared, sad, lonely, frustrated.

antipattern.symptom: Need field reads 'I need you to reply within 1h.' This is a request, not a need.
antipattern.fix: Separate: need = universal ('clarity'); request = specific ('reply within 4h').

antipattern.symptom: Author defaults to Collaborating on a low-stakes scheduling conflict; conversation drags 3 days.
antipattern.fix: Apply stakes×relationship matrix; low-low → Avoiding or quick Accommodating.

antipattern.symptom: Request reads 'stop being late.' Listener doesn't know what 'on-time' looks like.
antipattern.root-cause: Author specified what to stop, not what to do.
antipattern.fix: Reframe positively: 'arrive 5 min before standup' instead of 'stop being late'.
